example.context: Laravel 11 API; Order model + OrderItem child; need POST /api/v1/orders accepting items array; transactional create; Sanctum auth.
example.walk: Step 1: artisan generates Api\\V1\\OrderController + StoreOrderRequest + OrderResource + OrderPolicy. Step 2: StoreOrderRequest::rules returns ['items' => 'required|array|min:1', 'items.*.sku' => 'required|string', 'items.*.qty' => 'required|integer|min:1']. authorize returns $this->user()->can('create', Order::class). Step 3: OrderService::create(array $data) uses DB::transaction(function () use ($data) { $order = Order::create(['user_id' => auth()->id()]); foreach ($data['items'] as $i) $order->items()->create($i); return $order; }). Step 4: OrderResource::toArray returns ['id' => $this->id, 'items' => OrderItemResource::collection($this->whenLoaded('items')), 'created_at' => $this->created_at->toIso8601String()]. Step 5: Controller method 4 lines: return new OrderResource($this->service->create($request->validated()));. Step 6: OrderPolicy::create returns $user->subscribed(); registered in AuthServiceProvider.
example.output: Controller 32 lines total; tx scoped to service; Resource controls API shape; authorization via Policy; lint script clean.
